title: psychanthus bracteolatus
intro: Psychanthus bracteolatus, also known as the hairy bryony, is a type of vining plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Psychanthus bracteolatus include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, or used topically as a poultice to aid in the healing process.
